data.title: Bitwise CIO Says AI and Precious Metals Helped Spark Bitcoin Downturn, And Several Catalysts Could ‘Turn Things Around’
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) is now nearly 50% below its all-time high set four months ago. And according to Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan, there is “never a single reason why the crypto market falls,” but rather multiple factors at work. Hougan believes there are six major drivers behind the downturn
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/02/16/bitwise-cio-says-ai-and-precious-metals-helped-spark-bitcoin-downturn-and-several-catalysts-could-turn-things-around

data.title: Bank of England's Rate Cut Predictions Vary Among Economists
data.description: Economists are divided on the number of interest rate cuts the Bank of England might implement this year. According to Jin10, a Reuters survey reveals that 18 economists anticipate one to two rate cuts, while 16 predict two to three cuts. This divergence in opinion highlights the uncertainty surrounding the central bank's monetary policy decisions amid ongoing economic challenges.

data.title: Economists Predict Higher UK CPI in Q2, Diverging from Bank of England's Forecast
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Reuters reveals that 22 out of 30 economists anticipate the UK's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for the second quarter to surpass the projections made in the Bank of England's February monetary policy report. According to Jin10, these economists hold a more hawkish stance on inflation compared to the central bank. The divergence in expectations highlights ongoing concerns about inflationary pressures in the UK economy.

data.title: Market Liquidity: Potential for Cash Inflow into Crypto Amid Low Cash Reserves
data.description: Retail investors, mutual funds, and professional fund managers are currently maintaining historically low cash reserves, according to NS3.AI. This situation limits their capacity to capitalize on market dips. However, there is still $7.7 trillion held in money market funds, which represents potential liquidity that could be redirected into risk assets like Bitcoin if short-term yields decrease. The market's delicate state indicates that the next significant catalyst could lead to heightened volatility and a possible reallocation of cash into the cryptocurrency sector.

data.title: deBridge launches MCP enabling AI agents to execute non-custodial cross-chain transactions.
data.description: Cross-chain protocol deBridge has announced the launch of its Model Context Protocol (MCP), allowing AI agents and developer tools to perform exchanges, bridges, and multi-step transactions between the EVM chain and Solana. This release follows the launch of deBridge Bundles in December, an intent-based execution model designed to eliminate chain-level complexity. (The Block)

data.title: Over $26 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network in the past hour, with $12.92 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, approximately $26.01 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $12.92 million in BTC and approximately $7.02 million in ETH.

data.title: South Korean police have confirmed that 22 BTC involved in the case have been leaked, and the internal circulation path and the possibility of personnel involvement are under investigation.
data.description: Twenty-two bitcoins held by the Gangnam Police Station in South Korea have been confirmed to have been transferred out of the country, worth approximately 2.1 billion won at current prices. These bitcoins were voluntarily submitted assets received by police during an investigation in 2021. The investigation revealed that the physical cold wallet was not stolen, but the assets within it were transferred out. Law enforcement agencies are investigating the flow of these bitcoins and the possibility of insider involvement. This discovery reportedly occurred during a nationwide search following the disclosure of the theft of 320 bitcoins by the Gwangju District Prosecutors' Office. (Donga)

data.title: CLARITY Act Sparks Debate Over Digital Dollar Holding Fees
data.description: The CLARITY Act is at the center of a debate concerning fees for holding a 'digital dollar' in the United States. According to Bitalk News, banks and policymakers are at an impasse over stablecoin yields, which is affecting the progress of the legislation. Recent discussions between the White House and the banking sector have failed to resolve the deadlock, with banks expressing caution regarding stablecoin yields. The future of the act remains uncertain.

data.title: Curvance Prevents Hacking Attempt on DeFi Platform
data.description: Curvance, a multi-chain decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol, successfully prevented a hacking attempt on its front end, ensuring no funds were lost. According to NS3.AI, the platform's security partner identified the threat early, allowing for swift preventive measures to secure the system. Users have been advised to refrain from using the front end while a security investigation is underway.

data.title: X Introduces 'Smart Cashtags' for Real-Time Crypto and Stock Data
data.description: X has unveiled 'Smart Cashtags,' a new feature designed to deliver real-time data for cryptocurrencies and stocks directly within user posts. According to NS3.AI, this initiative seeks to improve user engagement by integrating live market information into social media interactions. The development marks a significant step towards merging financial data with social media platforms, offering users immediate access to market trends and insights.

data.title: Leadership Change at Ethereum Foundation as Tomasz Stanczak Steps Down
data.description: Tomasz Stanczak has announced his resignation from the Ethereum Foundation, signaling a notable shift in the organization's leadership. According to NS3.AI, the departure of Stanczak raises questions about the future direction of Ethereum's development. However, details regarding his successor or the potential impact on Ethereum's projects have not been revealed.

data.title: Chancellor's EU Relations May Prompt Policy Reversal
data.description: The Chancellor of the Exchequer's ongoing criticism of Britain's stringent environmental protections may face a significant challenge. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that her efforts to improve relations with the European Union could necessitate a reconsideration of her stance. This potential policy shift highlights the complex balance between domestic priorities and international diplomacy. The Chancellor's approach to environmental regulations has been a point of contention, and aligning with EU standards could lead to a notable policy adjustment. The situation underscores the intricate dynamics at play in the Chancellor's efforts to navigate both national and European interests.

data.title: Apollo Partners with Morpho to Expand DeFi Lending Services
data.description: Apollo has entered the decentralized finance (DeFi) lending sector through a partnership with Morpho, a platform recognized for its tokenized lending services. According to NS3.AI, this strategic alliance highlights Apollo's ambition to utilize DeFi technologies to broaden its financial services. The collaboration is expected to boost the adoption and integration of DeFi lending protocols within traditional financial systems.

data.title: Fortress Lending Fund V Expands to Attract European Investors
data.description: The alternative asset manager is enhancing its Fortress Lending Fund V by introducing an unleveraged component. Bloomberg posted on X that this strategic move aims to attract insurance companies and other European institutional investors. The addition is designed to broaden the fund's appeal and diversify its investor base, aligning with the firm's growth objectives in the European market.

data.title: Is crypto infrastructure ready for neobanks?
data.description:
Crypto infrastructure has shown a willingness to carry out bank-like operations, leading to a trend of neobank launches. Recent conflicts, however, raise the issue of crypto’s readiness to host neobanks. Crypto networks have been proposed as venues for neobanks multiple times. The rise of crypto payments also coincided with other digital neobanks, which used conventional […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/is-crypto-infrastructure-ready-for-neobanks/

data.title: Bitcoin Losses in South Korean Police Stations Raise Security Concerns
data.description: South Korean police have reported the disappearance of nearly $2 million in Bitcoin stored on USB drives within a police station. According to NS3.AI, this incident has prompted security experts to call for enhanced management of crypto assets. The situation follows another case in Gwangju, where prosecutors acknowledged losing $28 million in Bitcoin due to a phishing scam. Investigations are underway, suggesting potential insider involvement and underscoring significant vulnerabilities in law enforcement's handling of digital assets.
